upstream_status.summary: Synced 9f2e0bd0ea→b8be62ffac: applied HTML-report rewrite to improve-codebase-architecture/SKILL.md (manual merge preserving glossary re-pointings); added HTML-REPORT.md from upstream; kept local grill-with-docs/SKILL.md (forge-keeper adaptations).

customizations.reason: Tied to Matt Pocock's own setup, not promoted for redistribution per upstream CLAUDE.md
Upstream marks these as no longer used

customizations.summary: Excluded misc bucket — not part of curated subset
customizations.reason: User selected only 6 skills (grill-me, grill-with-docs, to-prd, tdd, diagnose, triage)

Each ported skill is self-contained. Setup info (canonical triage labels, issue-tracker assumptions) is inlined in the skills that need it (triage). to-prd no longer references an issue tracker at all.

customizations.summary: Added on second-pass review. Re-pointed CONTEXT.md / CONTEXT-FORMAT.md references at forge-keeper docs structure (docs/glossary.md / GLOSSARY-FORMAT.md). LANGUAGE.md and DEEPENING.md copied unmodified; INTERFACE-DESIGN.md had one CONTEXT.md mention, now generic.
customizations.reason: Completes the engineering trinity (build → diagnose → improve). Architecture vocabulary aligns with the deep-modules concept already in the tdd skill.
Tiny skill (4 lines of body) that fills the 'help me understand this area' niche which the rest of the framework doesn't cover
User did not request this skill — forge-proactive-qa already covers QA in dev-forge
Productivity skill: ultra-compressed communication mode. Self-contained, opt-in via explicit triggers ('caveman mode' / 'be brief'); no risk of accidental activation
skill-creator (already used in this account) is the more sophisticated of the two. Two skills competing for the same triggers worsens activation for both.

customizations.summary: Excluded misc bucket (git-guardrails, migrate-to-shoehorn, scaffold-exercises, setup-pre-commit)
customizations.reason: git-guardrails-claude-code overlaps with forge-security + forge-hookify (the latter exists precisely to author rules like this). setup-pre-commit is Husky/JS-only, too narrow for dev-forge's multi-stack profile. migrate-to-shoehorn and scaffold-exercises are Pocock-specific tooling (TS test library and course scaffolding).

Upstream targets CONTEXT.md / CONTEXT-MAP.md. Dev Forge projects center docs around CLAUDE.md (root + zoned), .claude/rules/, docs/exemplars.md, docs/adr/, and (lazy) docs/glossary.md. The skill now grills against that layout.

customizations.summary: Removed — replaced by GLOSSARY-FORMAT.md aligned with forge-keeper docs
customizations.reason: Forge-keeper keeps domain language in docs/glossary.md (lazy), separate from CLAUDE.md's conventions/architecture role (which is line-limited)

customizations.summary: Removed all GitHub / issue-tracker assignment; output is a wave-organized plan saved to docs/plans/
customizations.reason: User explicitly requested no GitHub assignment and plans organized by waves like forge-superpowers (parallel tasks within a wave, sequential between waves)

customizations.summary: Excluded triage skill — fundamentally an issue-tracker workflow
customizations.reason: User opted out: without an issue tracker pumping work in, the skill has nothing to triage. The state machine and AGENT-BRIEF / OUT-OF-SCOPE patterns are independently useful but not bundled here.

customizations.summary: Added README with attribution to Matt Pocock and per-skill manifest
customizations.reason: Required by dev-forge curation conventions

Interactive design exploration overlaps with forge-ui-forge, which is already the dev-forge tool for prototyping screens, registry components, and per-project mock fixtures with hot-reload annotation. Two skills competing on prototyping triggers worsens activation for both.

Agent transition documentation overlaps with forge-keeper:sync + docs/sessions/ logs, which already capture session handoffs in dev-forge. Different mental model — handoff writes a single file targeted at the next agent, forge-keeper persists structured session entries.

Two-axis code review overlaps with forge-deep-review (5 specialist review agents) and forge-superpowers requesting-code-review. Adding a third reviewer worsens trigger competition without adding a perspective dev-forge doesn't already cover.
Marked in-progress upstream; niche writing-workflow tool (capture initial user prompts as fragments) not aligned with dev-forge's engineering-oriented plugin set
Marked in-progress upstream; writing-workflow tool, not engineering-oriented
Marked in-progress upstream; writing-workflow tool, not engineering-oriented

customizations.summary: Excluded upstream out-of-scope notes (added during 2026-04 → 2026-05 window)
customizations.reason: Upstream project's own audit trail of rejected skill ideas; not part of curated skill content
Upstream maintainer tooling for managing skill symlinks across mattpocock's monorepo; dev-forge's marketplace structure handles plugin discovery differently

customizations.summary: Replaced with dev-forge plugin metadata (name: forge-mattpocock, dev-forge author/repo, dev-forge-tracked version)
customizations.reason: Each dev-forge plugin is published under the forge-* namespace with its own author/version. Standard renaming pattern applied to every external plugin in this marketplace.

macOS Bash sandbox blocks /var/folders/.../T/ which $TMPDIR resolves to (documented in dev-forge CLAUDE.md gotchas); using project-local .tmp/ keeps writes inside the allowlisted project root. User is responsible for adding .tmp/ to .gitignore.
